---
name: Grammar reviewer — availability signal
about: Let the project know you are available to review grammar entries
title: "[grammar-review: available] "
labels: grammar-review, reviewer-onboarding
assignees: ''
---

**Before filing**: read `docs/grammar-review.md` to understand what review involves.

---

## About you

**Background** (pick one or describe):

- [ ] Native speaker of Japanese
- [ ] Near-native speaker (raised bilingual, or lived in Japan for 10+ years, or equivalent)
- [ ] University-level training in Japanese linguistics (specify: degree, institution, years)
- [ ] Teaching experience (specify: levels taught, years, institution / independent)
- [ ] JLPT N1 holder with strong reading fluency
- [ ] Other: _____

**Name or handle for credit** (optional): _____

You may use your real name, GitHub handle, or a pseudonym. You may also choose to remain uncredited — just say so and the project will not list you in README or in `reviewer_notes` on the entries you touch.

## Review track

Which track can you contribute to?

- [ ] `community_reviewed` — I am eligible (linguistics training / teaching experience / JLPT N1) but I am not a native speaker.
- [ ] `native_speaker_reviewed` — I am a native speaker (or near-native equivalent) and my review can mark entries as native-reviewed directly.

If you are unsure, err on the side of `community_reviewed` — the project will always welcome a `native_speaker_reviewed` upgrade later.

## Capacity

**Entries per month you are willing to review**: _____

A typical review is 3–5 minutes per entry if nothing is wrong, longer when there is a real issue. Reviewing 20 entries in one session is a reasonable first batch.

## Scope

**Which JLPT levels do you want to review?**

- [ ] N5 (foundational — most likely to be reviewed first)
- [ ] N4
- [ ] N3
- [ ] N2
- [ ] N1 (includes classical / literary patterns; needs specialist background)
- [ ] Any / no preference

**Any specific interest areas?** (e.g., keigo, classical literature, modern colloquial, verb conjugation)

## First batch

Would you like the project author to recommend a starting slice, or would you rather pick one yourself?

- [ ] Recommend one (default: an N5 or N4 batch that is fully within your declared capacity)
- [ ] I will pick one and file a `grammar-review-batch` issue

## Anything else

Free-form notes, questions, or context you want the project to know.
